questions.question: What are political institutions? Give one example.
questions.answer: Political institutions are organizations that help in governing a country and making decisions. Example: Parliament.

questions.question: Why are institutions important in a democracy?
questions.answer: Institutions ensure that decisions are taken according to rules and procedures and involve many people.

questions.question: What is Parliament?
questions.answer: Parliament is the highest law-making body in India that represents the people.

questions.question: Name the two houses of the Indian Parliament.
questions.answer: The two houses of Parliament are Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha.

questions.question: What is the function of Parliament?
questions.answer: The main function of Parliament is to make laws and control the government.

questions.question: Who elects the members of Lok Sabha?
questions.answer: The people of India elect the members of Lok Sabha.

questions.question: What is the term of the Lok Sabha?
questions.answer: The term of the Lok Sabha is five years.

questions.question: Why is Rajya Sabha called a permanent house?
questions.answer: Rajya Sabha is called a permanent house because it is never dissolved.

questions.question: Who is the head of the government in India?
questions.answer: The Prime Minister is the head of the government in India.

questions.question: Who appoints the Prime Minister of India?
questions.answer: The President appoints the Prime Minister.

questions.question: What is the Council of Ministers?
questions.answer: The Council of Ministers is a group of ministers headed by the Prime Minister to run the government.

questions.question: What is the executive?
questions.answer: The executive is the branch of government that implements laws and policies.

questions.question: Who are civil servants?
questions.answer: Civil servants are government officials who help in implementing policies and administration.

questions.question: Who is the head of the state in India?
questions.answer: The President is the head of the state.

questions.question: How is the President of India elected?
questions.answer: The President is elected by the elected MPs and MLAs.

questions.question: What is the judiciary?
questions.answer: The judiciary is the system of courts that interprets laws and settles disputes.

questions.question: Name the highest court in India.
questions.answer: The Supreme Court is the highest court in India.

questions.question: What is judicial review?
questions.answer: Judicial review is the power of courts to examine laws and declare them unconstitutional.

questions.question: What is Public Interest Litigation?
questions.answer: Public Interest Litigation is a legal action filed in court to protect public interest.

questions.question: What is the Cabinet?
questions.answer: The Cabinet is a group of senior ministers who take important government decisions.

questions.question: Who chairs the Cabinet meetings?
questions.answer: The Prime Minister chairs the Cabinet meetings.

questions.question: Which institution controls government money?
questions.answer: Parliament controls government money.

questions.question: Which house has more power in money matters?
questions.answer: Lok Sabha has more power in money matters.

questions.question: What happens if the Lok Sabha passes a no-confidence motion?
questions.answer: The Council of Ministers must resign.

questions.question: Why is an independent judiciary important?
questions.answer: An independent judiciary ensures fair justice and protects citizens' rights.
